title: French Berry Tart
description: A classic French dessert featuring a flaky crust, fresh berries, and a hint of mint. Perfect for warm weather gatherings.

ingredients.name: Fresh Berries (such as strawberries, blueberries, raspberries)

instructions: Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
Trim the edges and press the dough into the corners of the pan.
Prick the bottom of the crust with a fork to prevent it from bubbling up during baking.
Line the crust with parchment paper and fill with pie weights or dried beans.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Remove the parchment paper and pie weights or beans.
Arrange the fresh berries on top of the tart.
In a small bowl, mix together the granulated sugar and lemon juice.
Drizzle the sugar mixture over the berries.
Dust the tart with tart confectioners' sugar and garnish with mint leaves.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
